response: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), founded in 1861, is the top-ranked university in the world, with a highly selective acceptance rate of just 3.9%. It enrolls approximately 11,500 students and employs 1,069 faculty members, boasting an impressive 98 Nobel laureates among its affiliates. With an annual tuition of $57,590 and an endowment of $24.6 billion, MIT remains a leader in research, innovation, and academic excellence.
